theorem_statement:
Let
\[
S^n=\{x\in\mathbb R^{n+1}:\|x\|=1\}.
\]
For every continuous map \(f:S^n\to\mathbb R^n\), there exists \(x\in S^n\) such that
\[
f(x)=f(-x).
\]
raw_proof_text:
Assume for contradiction that no antipodal pair has the same image under \(f\). Thus
\[
f(x)\ne f(-x)
\]
for every \(x\in S^n\). Define
\[
g:S^n\to\mathbb R^n,
\qquad
g(x)=f(x)-f(-x).
\]
The map \(g\) is continuous because \(f\) is continuous and the antipodal map \(x\mapsto -x\) is continuous. It is also odd, since
\[
g(-x)=f(-x)-f(x)=-g(x).
\]
By the contradiction assumption, \(g(x)\ne 0\) for every \(x\in S^n\).

Since \(g\) never vanishes, define
\[
h:S^n\to S^{n-1},
\qquad
h(x)=\frac{g(x)}{\|g(x)\|}.
\]
This map is continuous because it is obtained from the continuous map \(g\) by dividing by the positive continuous function \(x\mapsto \|g(x)\|\). It is also odd:
\[
h(-x)=\frac{g(-x)}{\|g(-x)\|}
=\frac{-g(x)}{\|g(x)\|}
=-h(x).
\]
Thus the assumption produces a continuous odd map
\[
h:S^n\to S^{n-1}.
\]

We now use the standard no-odd-map theorem: there is no continuous odd map from \(S^n\) to \(S^{n-1}\). One proof is by passing to real projective spaces. If such an odd map \(h\) existed, then it would respect the antipodal equivalence relation and would descend to a continuous map
\[
\bar h:\mathbb RP^n\to \mathbb RP^{n-1}.
\]
Let \(a_k\in H^1(\mathbb RP^k;\mathbb F_2)\) be the standard generator. The mod-2 cohomology ring is
\[
H^*(\mathbb RP^k;\mathbb F_2)\cong \mathbb F_2[a_k]/(a_k^{k+1}).
\]
In particular, \(a_n^n\ne 0\) in \(H^n(\mathbb RP^n;\mathbb F_2)\), while \(a_{n-1}^n=0\) in \(H^n(\mathbb RP^{n-1};\mathbb F_2)\). The descended map \(\bar h\) pulls back the tautological double cover over \(\mathbb RP^{n-1}\) to the tautological double cover over \(\mathbb RP^n\). Therefore
\[
\bar h^*(a_{n-1})=a_n.
\]
Applying \(\bar h^*\) to the relation \(a_{n-1}^n=0\) gives
\[
0=\bar h^*(a_{n-1}^n)=(\bar h^*a_{n-1})^n=a_n^n,
\]
contradicting \(a_n^n\ne0\). Hence no continuous odd map \(S^n\to S^{n-1}\) exists.

The continuous odd map \(h:S^n\to S^{n-1}\) constructed above is therefore impossible. The contradiction shows that our assumption was false. Hence there must exist some \(x\in S^n\) such that
\[
f(x)=f(-x).
\]
This proves the Borsuk--Ulam theorem.
